brief_description: A fairy-tale city where Gothic spires pierce the skyline, Baroque palaces line cobblestoned streets, and the Vltava River reflects a thousand years of history. Prague emerged from the Cold War with its medieval core miraculously intact—Charles Bridge, the Astronomical Clock, and Prague Castle create a cityscape that feels lifted from a storybook. Add world-class beer at impossibly low prices, and you have one of Europe's most magical destinations.

sections.title: Why Prague
sections.content: Prague offers the grandeur of Vienna at a fraction of the cost, with better beer. The city's historic center—a UNESCO World Heritage site—survived World War II virtually untouched, leaving a continuous sweep of Gothic, Renaissance, Baroque, and Art Nouveau architecture. This isn't a reconstructed city; it's the real thing, from the 14th-century Charles Bridge to the Art Nouveau cafés of the early 1900s. Add a thriving food scene, legendary beer culture, and genuine value for money, and Prague delivers far more than its Instagram reputation suggests.

sections.title: The Neighborhoods
sections.content: Staré Město (Old Town) centers on the famous square with its Astronomical Clock and Gothic Týn Church—touristy but genuinely spectacular. Malá Strana (Lesser Town), below the castle, offers Baroque palaces and quiet gardens. Hradčany surrounds the castle complex itself. Nové Město (New Town), despite the name, dates to the 14th century and holds Wenceslas Square and the National Theatre. Vinohrady and Žižkov, beyond the tourist core, are where locals actually live—excellent restaurants, neighborhood pubs, and genuine Prague life.

sections.title: Getting Around
sections.content: The metro is fast, clean, and covers the main tourist areas with three lines. Trams are charming and useful—the 22 runs from the castle through Malá Strana and New Town. Walking is essential for the historic core; cobblestones are beautiful but challenging in heels. The city is compact enough that most sights are reachable on foot. Buy a 24-hour pass (120 CZK) or use contactless payment. Taxis are affordable; use Bolt or Liftago apps to avoid tourist-trap pricing.

why_visit: One of Europe's best-preserved medieval cities, with Gothic, Baroque, and Art Nouveau architecture
Prague Castle—the world's largest ancient castle complex, dominating the city skyline
World-class beer at €2-3 per half-liter, in pubs that treat brewing as high art
Exceptional value compared to Western Europe—top restaurants at half the price
Charles Bridge at sunrise, the Astronomical Clock, and the fairy-tale Old Town Square

practical_info.language: Czech (English widely spoken in tourist areas)
practical_info.tipping: 10% at restaurants; round up at bars and for taxis
practical_info.tap_water: Safe and excellent—Prague's tap water is high quality

seasonal_notes.spring: Blooming gardens, Easter markets, pleasant 12-18°C temperatures, manageable crowds
seasonal_notes.summer: Long days, outdoor terraces, festivals, but peak tourist season and occasional heat
seasonal_notes.fall: Golden leaves, harvest season, fewer crowds, crisp air—arguably the best time
seasonal_notes.winter: Magical Christmas markets, snow-dusted spires, cold but atmospheric

best_time_to_visit.overall: May-June and September-October for best weather and manageable crowds
best_time_to_visit.budget: November-March (excluding Christmas/New Year) for lowest prices
best_time_to_visit.weather: May-September for warmest weather and longest days
best_time_to_visit.events: Easter markets (spring), Signal Festival (October), Christmas markets (December)

Must Do.optimal_time: Watch the clock strike the hour
